001/**
002 * Application entry point for the Zuul-style Pokémon adventure game.
003 * <p>
004 * This class wires the {@link GameEngine} to the {@link UserInterface} so that
005 * player input is displayed and processed through a single {@code main}-less
006 * bootstrap (typical BlueJ style: instantiate {@code Game} to start).
007 * </p>
008 *
009 * @author Barnabe Jouanard
010 * @version 2026.02.17
011 */
012public class Game
013{
014    /** Swing-based view that shows text, images, and command controls. */
015    private UserInterface aGui;
016    /** Core game logic: rooms, parser, and command interpretation. */
017    private GameEngine aEngine;
018
019    /**
020     * Constructs the game by creating the engine and GUI, then linking them.
021     * <p>
022     * The GUI receives a reference to the engine; the engine is given the GUI
023     * so it can print messages and update the display after construction.
024     * </p>
025     */
026    public Game()
027    {
028        this.aEngine = new GameEngine();
029        this.aGui = new UserInterface( this.aEngine );
030        this.aEngine.setGUI( this.aGui );
031    } // Game()
032} // Game
